text:
There once was a little rabbit who lived in a warren deep in the forest. He loved his life. Every day, the sun rose over the hill, casting its golden light on his dew-speckled nose. There were butterflies in thousands, and the birds sang sweet melodies. He nibbled greens and hummed cheerful tunes.

There was trouble, though. Other rabbits started whispering about a hunter stalking the warren. One sleepy evening, he overheard their chatter. Panicked whispers turned into shrill warnings, and the little rabbit did his best to ignore them. Night fell, and the hunter came.

The rabbit froze. His heart pounded. The hunter was big and scary. But he hadn't seen anything. They exchanged polite glances before quietly walking away. The rabbit returned to his daytime dreams of hoppy adventures.

The next day, he felt different. He wasn't just scared; he felt vulnerable, exposed. He knew he needed a secret retreat—a place no hunter could
